1.text: The words<S>1697</S> of Amos<S>5986</S>, who was among the herdmen<S>5349</S> of Tekoa<S>8620</S>, which he saw<S>2372</S> concerning Israel<S>3478</S> in the days<S>3117</S> of Uzziah<S>5818</S> king<S>4428</S> of Judah<S>3063</S>, and in the days<S>3117</S> of Jeroboam<S>3379</S> the son<S>1121</S> of Joash<S>3101</S> king<S>4428</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, two years<S>8141</S> before<S>6440</S> the earthquake<S>7494</S>.

And he said<S>559</S>, The LORD<S>3068</S> will roar<S>7580</S> from Zion<S>6726</S>, and utter<S>5414</S> his voice<S>6963</S> from Jerusalem<S>3389</S>; and the habitations<S>4999</S> of the shepherds<S>7462</S> shall mourn<S>56</S>, and the top<S>7218</S> of Carmel<S>3760</S> shall wither<S>3001</S>.

Thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; For three<S>7969</S> transgressions<S>6588</S> of Damascus<S>1834</S>, and for four<S>702</S>, I will not turn away<S>7725</S> the punishment thereof; because they have threshed<S>1758</S> Gilead<S>1568</S> with threshing instruments<S>2742</S> of iron<S>1270</S>: <sup>and: or, yea, for four</sup> <sup>turn: or, convert it, or, let it be quiet</sup>
But I will send<S>7971</S> a fire<S>784</S> into the house<S>1004</S> of Hazael<S>2371</S>, which shall devour<S>398</S> the palaces<S>759</S> of Benhadad<S>1130</S>.

I will break<S>7665</S> also the bar<S>1280</S> of Damascus<S>1834</S>, and cut off<S>3772</S> the inhabitant<S>3427</S> from the plain<S>1237</S> of Aven<S>206</S>, and him that holdeth<S>8551</S> the sceptre<S>7626</S> from the house<S>1004</S> of Eden<S>5731</S> <S>1040</S>: and the people<S>5971</S> of Syria<S>758</S> shall go into captivity<S>1540</S> unto Kir<S>7024</S>, saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>. <sup>the plain: or, Bikathaven</sup> <sup>the house: or, Betheden</sup>

Thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; For three<S>7969</S> transgressions<S>6588</S> of Gaza<S>5804</S>, and for four<S>702</S>, I will not turn away<S>7725</S> the punishment thereof; because they carried away captive<S>1540</S> the whole<S>8003</S> captivity<S>1546</S>, to deliver them up<S>5462</S> to Edom<S>123</S>: <sup>away captive: or, them away with an entire</sup>
But I will send<S>7971</S> a fire<S>784</S> on the wall<S>2346</S> of Gaza<S>5804</S>, which shall devour<S>398</S> the palaces<S>759</S> thereof:

And I will cut off<S>3772</S> the inhabitant<S>3427</S> from Ashdod<S>795</S>, and him that holdeth<S>8551</S> the sceptre<S>7626</S> from Ashkelon<S>831</S>, and I will turn<S>7725</S> mine hand<S>3027</S> against Ekron<S>6138</S>: and the remnant<S>7611</S> of the Philistines<S>6430</S> shall perish<S>6</S>, saith<S>559</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>.

Thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; For three<S>7969</S> transgressions<S>6588</S> of Tyrus<S>6865</S>, and for four<S>702</S>, I will not turn away<S>7725</S> the punishment thereof; because they delivered up<S>5462</S> the whole<S>8003</S> captivity<S>1546</S> to Edom<S>123</S>, and remembered<S>2142</S> not the brotherly<S>251</S> covenant<S>1285</S>: <sup>the brotherly: Heb. the covenant of brethren</sup>
But I will send<S>7971</S> a fire<S>784</S> on the wall<S>2346</S> of Tyrus<S>6865</S>, which shall devour<S>398</S> the palaces<S>759</S> thereof.

Thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; For three<S>7969</S> transgressions<S>6588</S> of Edom<S>123</S>, and for four<S>702</S>, I will not turn away<S>7725</S> the punishment thereof; because he did pursue<S>7291</S> his brother<S>251</S> with the sword<S>2719</S>, and did cast off<S>7843</S> all pity<S>7356</S>, and his anger<S>639</S> did tear<S>2963</S> perpetually<S>5703</S>, and he kept<S>8104</S> his wrath<S>5678</S> for ever<S>5331</S>: <sup>did cast: Heb. corrupted his compassions</sup>
But I will send<S>7971</S> a fire<S>784</S> upon Teman<S>8487</S>, which shall devour<S>398</S> the palaces<S>759</S> of Bozrah<S>1224</S>.

Thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; For three<S>7969</S> transgressions<S>6588</S> of the children<S>1121</S> of Ammon<S>5983</S>, and for four<S>702</S>, I will not turn away<S>7725</S> the punishment thereof; because they have ripped up<S>1234</S> the women with child<S>2030</S> of Gilead<S>1568</S>, that they might enlarge<S>7337</S> their border<S>1366</S>: <sup>ripped: or, divided the mountains</sup>

But I will kindle<S>3341</S> a fire<S>784</S> in the wall<S>2346</S> of Rabbah<S>7237</S>, and it shall devour<S>398</S> the palaces<S>759</S> thereof, with shouting<S>8643</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of battle<S>4421</S>, with a tempest<S>5591</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of the whirlwind<S>5492</S>:
And their king<S>4428</S> shall go<S>1980</S> into captivity<S>1473</S>, he and his princes<S>8269</S> together<S>3162</S>, saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.

2.text: Thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; For three<S>7969</S> transgressions<S>6588</S> of Moab<S>4124</S>, and for four<S>702</S>, I will not turn away<S>7725</S> the punishment thereof; because he burned<S>8313</S> the bones<S>6106</S> of the king<S>4428</S> of Edom<S>123</S> into lime<S>7875</S>:

But I will send<S>7971</S> a fire<S>784</S> upon Moab<S>4124</S>, and it shall devour<S>398</S> the palaces<S>759</S> of Kerioth<S>7152</S>: and Moab<S>4124</S> shall die<S>4191</S> with tumult<S>7588</S>, with shouting<S>8643</S>, and with the sound<S>6963</S> of the trumpet<S>7782</S>:
And I will cut off<S>3772</S> the judge<S>8199</S> from the midst<S>7130</S> thereof, and will slay<S>2026</S> all the princes<S>8269</S> thereof with him, saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.

Thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; For three<S>7969</S> transgressions<S>6588</S> of Judah<S>3063</S>, and for four<S>702</S>, I will not turn away<S>7725</S> the punishment thereof; because they have despised<S>3988</S> the law<S>8451</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>, and have not kept<S>8104</S> his commandments<S>2706</S>, and their lies<S>3577</S> caused them to err<S>8582</S>, after<S>310</S> the which their fathers<S>1</S> have walked<S>1980</S>:
But I will send<S>7971</S> a fire<S>784</S> upon Judah<S>3063</S>, and it shall devour<S>398</S> the palaces<S>759</S> of Jerusalem<S>3389</S>.

Thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; For three<S>7969</S> transgressions<S>6588</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, and for four<S>702</S>, I will not turn away<S>7725</S> the punishment thereof; because they sold<S>4376</S> the righteous<S>6662</S> for silver<S>3701</S>, and the poor<S>34</S> for a pair of shoes<S>5275</S>;

That pant<S>7602</S> after the dust<S>6083</S> of the earth<S>776</S> on the head<S>7218</S> of the poor<S>1800</S>, and turn aside<S>5186</S> the way<S>1870</S> of the meek<S>6035</S>: and a man<S>376</S> and his father<S>1</S> will go<S>3212</S> in unto the same maid<S>5291</S>, to profane<S>2490</S> my holy<S>6944</S> name<S>8034</S>: <sup>maid: or, young woman</sup>

And they lay themselves down<S>5186</S> upon clothes<S>899</S> laid to pledge<S>2254</S> by<S>681</S> every altar<S>4196</S>, and they drink<S>8354</S> the wine<S>3196</S> of the condemned<S>6064</S> in the house<S>1004</S> of their god<S>430</S>. <sup>the condemned: or, such as have fined, or, mulcted</sup>

Yet destroyed<S>8045</S> I the Amorite<S>567</S> before<S>6440</S> them, whose height<S>1363</S> was like the height<S>1363</S> of the cedars<S>730</S>, and he was strong<S>2634</S> as the oaks<S>437</S>; yet I destroyed<S>8045</S> his fruit<S>6529</S> from above<S>4605</S>, and his roots<S>8328</S> from beneath.

Also I brought you up<S>5927</S> from the land<S>776</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>, and led<S>3212</S> you forty<S>705</S> years<S>8141</S> through the wilderness<S>4057</S>, to possess<S>3423</S> the land<S>776</S> of the Amorite<S>567</S>.

And I raised up<S>6965</S> of your sons<S>1121</S> for prophets<S>5030</S>, and of your young men<S>970</S> for Nazarites<S>5139</S>. Is it not even thus, O ye children<S>1121</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>? saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.
But ye gave the Nazarites<S>5139</S> wine<S>3196</S> to drink<S>8248</S>; and commanded<S>6680</S> the prophets<S>5030</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Prophesy<S>5012</S> not.

Behold, I am pressed<S>5781</S> under you, as a cart<S>5699</S> is pressed<S>5781</S> that is full<S>4392</S> of sheaves<S>5995</S>. <sup>I am: or, I will press your place, as a cart full of sheaves presseth</sup>

Therefore the flight<S>4498</S> shall perish<S>6</S> from the swift<S>7031</S>, and the strong<S>2389</S> shall not strengthen<S>553</S> his force<S>3581</S>, neither shall the mighty<S>1368</S> deliver<S>4422</S> himself<S>5315</S>: <sup>himself: Heb. his soul, or, life</sup>

Neither shall he stand<S>5975</S> that handleth<S>8610</S> the bow<S>7198</S>; and he that is swift<S>7031</S> of foot<S>7272</S> shall not deliver<S>4422</S> himself: neither shall he that rideth<S>7392</S> the horse<S>5483</S> deliver<S>4422</S> himself<S>5315</S>.

And he that is courageous<S>533</S> <S>3820</S> among the mighty<S>1368</S> shall flee away<S>5127</S> naked<S>6174</S> in that day<S>3117</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>. <sup>courageous: Heb. strong of his heart</sup>

3.text: Hear<S>8085</S> this word<S>1697</S> that the LORD<S>3068</S> hath spoken<S>1696</S> against you, O children<S>1121</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, against the whole family<S>4940</S> which I brought up<S>5927</S> from the land<S>776</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>, saying<S>559</S>,
You only have I known<S>3045</S> of all the families<S>4940</S> of the earth<S>127</S>: therefore I will punish<S>6485</S> you for all your iniquities<S>5771</S>. <sup>punish: Heb. visit upon</sup>
Can two<S>8147</S> walk<S>3212</S> together<S>3162</S>, except<S>1115</S> they be agreed<S>3259</S>?

Will a lion<S>738</S> roar<S>7580</S> in the forest<S>3293</S>, when he hath no prey<S>2964</S>? will a young lion<S>3715</S> cry out<S>5414</S> <S>6963</S> of his den<S>4585</S>, if he have taken<S>3920</S> nothing<S>1115</S>? <sup>cry: Heb. give forth his voice</sup>

Can a bird<S>6833</S> fall<S>5307</S> in a snare<S>6341</S> upon the earth<S>776</S>, where no gin<S>4170</S> is for him? shall one take up<S>5927</S> a snare<S>6341</S> from the earth<S>127</S>, and have taken<S>3920</S> nothing at all<S>3920</S>?

Shall a trumpet<S>7782</S> be blown<S>8628</S> in the city<S>5892</S>, and the people<S>5971</S> not be afraid<S>2729</S>? shall there be evil<S>7451</S> in a city<S>5892</S>, and the LORD<S>3068</S> hath not done<S>6213</S> it? <sup>be afraid: or, run together? the LORD: or, shall not the L.doe somewhat?</sup>
Surely the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> will do<S>6213</S> nothing<S>1697</S>, but he revealeth<S>1540</S> his secret<S>5475</S> unto his servants<S>5650</S> the prophets<S>5030</S>.
The lion<S>738</S> hath roared<S>7580</S>, who will not<S>3808</S> fear<S>3372</S>? the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> hath spoken<S>1696</S>, who can but prophesy<S>5012</S>?

Publish<S>8085</S> in the palaces<S>759</S> at Ashdod<S>795</S>, and in the palaces<S>759</S> in the land<S>776</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>, and say<S>559</S>, Assemble<S>622</S> yourselves upon the mountains<S>2022</S> of Samaria<S>8111</S>, and behold<S>7200</S> the great<S>7227</S> tumults<S>4103</S> in the midst<S>7130</S> thereof, and the oppressed<S>6217</S> in the midst<S>8432</S> thereof. <sup>oppressed: or, oppressions</sup>

For they know<S>3045</S> not to do<S>6213</S> right<S>5229</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, who store<S>686</S> up violence<S>2555</S> and robbery<S>7701</S> in their palaces<S>759</S>. <sup>robbery: or, spoil</sup>

Therefore thus saith<S>559</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>; An adversary<S>6862</S> there shall be even round about<S>5439</S> the land<S>776</S>; and he shall bring down<S>3381</S> thy strength<S>5797</S> from thee, and thy palaces<S>759</S> shall be spoiled<S>962</S>.

Thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>; As the shepherd<S>7462</S> taketh out<S>5337</S> of the mouth<S>6310</S> of the lion<S>738</S> two<S>8147</S> legs<S>3767</S>, or a piece<S>915</S> of an ear<S>241</S>; so shall the children<S>1121</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> be taken out<S>5337</S> that dwell<S>3427</S> in Samaria<S>8111</S> in the corner<S>6285</S> of a bed<S>4296</S>, and in Damascus<S>1833</S> in a couch<S>6210</S>. <sup>taketh: Heb. delivereth</sup> <sup>in Damascus: or, on the bed's feet</sup>
Hear<S>8085</S> ye, and testify<S>5749</S> in the house<S>1004</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>, the God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>,

That in the day<S>3117</S> that I shall visit<S>6485</S> the transgressions<S>6588</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> upon him I will also visit<S>6485</S> the altars<S>4196</S> of Bethel<S>1008</S>: and the horns<S>7161</S> of the altar<S>4196</S> shall be cut off<S>1438</S>, and fall<S>5307</S> to the ground<S>776</S>. <sup>visit: or, punish Israel for</sup>

And I will smite<S>5221</S> the winter<S>2779</S> house<S>1004</S> with the summer<S>7019</S> house<S>1004</S>; and the houses<S>1004</S> of ivory<S>8127</S> shall perish<S>6</S>, and the great<S>7227</S> houses<S>1004</S> shall have an end<S>5486</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.

4.text: Hear<S>8085</S> this word<S>1697</S>, ye kine<S>6510</S> of Bashan<S>1316</S>, that are in the mountain<S>2022</S> of Samaria<S>8111</S>, which oppress<S>6231</S> the poor<S>1800</S>, which crush<S>7533</S> the needy<S>34</S>, which say<S>559</S> to their masters<S>113</S>, Bring<S>935</S>, and let us drink<S>8354</S>.

The Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> hath sworn<S>7650</S> by his holiness<S>6944</S>, that, lo, the days<S>3117</S> shall come<S>935</S> upon you, that he will take you away<S>5375</S> with hooks<S>6793</S>, and your posterity<S>319</S> with fishhooks<S>1729</S> <S>5518</S>.

And ye shall go out<S>3318</S> at the breaches<S>6556</S>, every<S>802</S> cow at that which is before her; and ye shall cast<S>7993</S> them into the palace<S>2038</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>. <sup>cast: or, cast away the things of the palace</sup>

Come<S>935</S> to Bethel<S>1008</S>, and transgress<S>6586</S>; at Gilgal<S>1537</S> multiply<S>7235</S> transgression<S>6586</S>; and bring<S>935</S> your sacrifices<S>2077</S> every morning<S>1242</S>, and your tithes<S>4643</S> after three<S>7969</S> years<S>3117</S>: <sup>three: Heb. three years of days</sup>

And offer<S>6999</S> a sacrifice of thanksgiving<S>8426</S> with leaven<S>2557</S>, and proclaim<S>7121</S> and publish<S>8085</S> the free offerings<S>5071</S>: for this<S>3651</S> liketh<S>157</S> you, O ye children<S>1121</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>. <sup>offer: Heb. offer by burning</sup> <sup>this: Heb. so ye love</sup>

And I also have given<S>5414</S> you cleanness<S>5356</S> of teeth<S>8127</S> in all your cities<S>5892</S>, and want<S>2640</S> of bread<S>3899</S> in all your places<S>4725</S>: yet have ye not returned<S>7725</S> unto me, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.

And also I have withholden<S>4513</S> the rain<S>1653</S> from you, when there were yet three<S>7969</S> months<S>2320</S> to the harvest<S>7105</S>: and I caused it to rain<S>4305</S> upon one<S>259</S> city<S>5892</S>, and caused it not to rain<S>4305</S> upon another<S>259</S> city<S>5892</S>: one<S>259</S> piece<S>2513</S> was rained<S>4305</S> upon, and the piece<S>2513</S> whereupon it rained<S>4305</S> not withered<S>3001</S>.

So two<S>8147</S> or three<S>7969</S> cities<S>5892</S> wandered<S>5128</S> unto one<S>259</S> city<S>5892</S>, to drink<S>8354</S> water<S>4325</S>; but they were not satisfied<S>7646</S>: yet have ye not returned<S>7725</S> unto me, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.

I have smitten<S>5221</S> you with blasting<S>7711</S> and mildew<S>3420</S>: when your gardens<S>1593</S> and your vineyards<S>3754</S> and your fig trees<S>8384</S> and your olive trees<S>2132</S> increased<S>7235</S>, the palmerworm<S>1501</S> devoured<S>398</S> them: yet have ye not returned<S>7725</S> unto me, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>. <sup>when: or, the multitude of your gardens, etc. did the palmerworm</sup>

I have sent<S>7971</S> among you the pestilence<S>1698</S> after the manner<S>1870</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>: your young men<S>970</S> have I slain<S>2026</S> with the sword<S>2719</S>, and have taken away<S>7628</S> your horses<S>5483</S>; and I have made the stink<S>889</S> of your camps<S>4264</S> to come up<S>5927</S> unto your nostrils<S>639</S>: yet have ye not returned<S>7725</S> unto me, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>. <sup>after: or, in the way</sup> <sup>and have: Heb. with the captivity of your horses</sup>

I have overthrown<S>2015</S> some of you, as God<S>430</S> overthrew<S>4114</S> Sodom<S>5467</S> and Gomorrah<S>6017</S>, and ye were as a firebrand<S>181</S> plucked out<S>5337</S> of the burning<S>8316</S>: yet have ye not returned<S>7725</S> unto me, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.

Therefore thus will I do<S>6213</S> unto thee, O Israel<S>3478</S>: and because<S>6118</S> I will do<S>6213</S> this unto thee, prepare<S>3559</S> to meet<S>7125</S> thy God<S>430</S>, O Israel<S>3478</S>.

For, lo, he that formeth<S>3335</S> the mountains<S>2022</S>, and createth<S>1254</S> the wind<S>7307</S>, and declareth<S>5046</S> unto man<S>120</S> what is his thought<S>7808</S>, that maketh<S>6213</S> the morning<S>7837</S> darkness<S>5890</S>, and treadeth<S>1869</S> upon the high places<S>1116</S> of the earth<S>776</S>, The LORD<S>3068</S>, The God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, is his name<S>8034</S>. <sup>wind: or, spirit</sup>

5.text: Hear<S>8085</S> ye this word<S>1697</S> which I take up<S>5375</S> against you, even a lamentation<S>7015</S>, O house<S>1004</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>.

The virgin<S>1330</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> is fallen<S>5307</S>; she shall no more<S>3254</S> rise<S>6965</S>: she is forsaken<S>5203</S> upon her land<S>127</S>; there is none to raise her up<S>6965</S>.

For thus saith<S>559</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>; The city<S>5892</S> that went out<S>3318</S> by a thousand<S>505</S> shall leave<S>7604</S> an hundred<S>3967</S>, and that which went forth<S>3318</S> by an hundred<S>3967</S> shall leave<S>7604</S> ten<S>6235</S>, to the house<S>1004</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>.
For thus saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> unto the house<S>1004</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, Seek<S>1875</S> ye me, and ye shall live<S>2421</S>:

But seek<S>1875</S> not Bethel<S>1008</S>, nor enter<S>935</S> into Gilgal<S>1537</S>, and pass<S>5674</S> not to Beersheba<S>884</S>: for Gilgal<S>1537</S> shall surely<S>1540</S> go into captivity<S>1540</S>, and Bethel<S>1008</S> shall come to nought<S>205</S>.

Seek<S>1875</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, and ye shall live<S>2421</S>; lest he break out<S>6743</S> like fire<S>784</S> in the house<S>1004</S> of Joseph<S>3130</S>, and devour<S>398</S> it, and there be none to quench<S>3518</S> it in Bethel<S>1008</S>.
Ye who turn<S>2015</S> judgment<S>4941</S> to wormwood<S>3939</S>, and leave off<S>3240</S> righteousness<S>6666</S> in the earth<S>776</S>,

Seek him that maketh<S>6213</S> the seven stars<S>3598</S> and Orion<S>3685</S>, and turneth<S>2015</S> the shadow of death<S>6757</S> into the morning<S>1242</S>, and maketh the day<S>3117</S> dark<S>2821</S> with night<S>3915</S>: that calleth<S>7121</S> for the waters<S>4325</S> of the sea<S>3220</S>, and poureth them out<S>8210</S> upon the face<S>6440</S> of the earth<S>776</S>: The LORD<S>3068</S> is his name<S>8034</S>:
That strengtheneth<S>1082</S> the spoiled<S>7701</S> against the strong<S>5794</S>, so that the spoiled<S>7701</S> shall come<S>935</S> against the fortress<S>4013</S>. <sup>spoiled: Heb. spoil</sup>
They hate<S>8130</S> him that rebuketh<S>3198</S> in the gate<S>8179</S>, and they abhor<S>8581</S> him that speaketh<S>1696</S> uprightly<S>8549</S>.

Forasmuch<S>3282</S> therefore as your treading<S>1318</S> is upon the poor<S>1800</S>, and ye take<S>3947</S> from him burdens<S>4864</S> of wheat<S>1250</S>: ye have built<S>1129</S> houses<S>1004</S> of hewn stone<S>1496</S>, but ye shall not dwell<S>3427</S> in them; ye have planted<S>5193</S> pleasant<S>2531</S> vineyards<S>3754</S>, but ye shall not drink<S>8354</S> wine<S>3196</S> of them. <sup>pleasant: Heb. vineyards of desire</sup>

For I know<S>3045</S> your manifold<S>7227</S> transgressions<S>6588</S> and your mighty<S>6099</S> sins<S>2403</S>: they afflict<S>6887</S> the just<S>6662</S>, they take<S>3947</S> a bribe<S>3724</S>, and they turn aside<S>5186</S> the poor<S>34</S> in the gate<S>8179</S> from their right. <sup>a bribe: or, a ransom</sup>
Therefore the prudent<S>7919</S> shall keep silence<S>1826</S> in that time<S>6256</S>; for it is an evil<S>7451</S> time<S>6256</S>.

Seek<S>1875</S> good<S>2896</S>, and not evil<S>7451</S>, that ye may live<S>2421</S>: and so the LORD<S>3068</S>, the God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, shall be with you, as ye have spoken<S>559</S>.

Hate<S>8130</S> the evil<S>7451</S>, and love<S>157</S> the good<S>2896</S>, and establish<S>3322</S> judgment<S>4941</S> in the gate<S>8179</S>: it may be that the LORD<S>3068</S> God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S> will be gracious<S>2603</S> unto the remnant<S>7611</S> of Joseph<S>3130</S>.

Therefore the LORD<S>3068</S>, the God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, the Lord<S>136</S>, saith<S>559</S> thus; Wailing<S>4553</S> shall be in all streets<S>7339</S>; and they shall say<S>559</S> in all the highways<S>2351</S>, Alas<S>1930</S>! alas<S>1930</S>! and they shall call<S>7121</S> the husbandman<S>406</S> to mourning<S>60</S>, and such as are skilful<S>3045</S> of lamentation<S>5092</S> to wailing<S>4553</S>.
And in all vineyards<S>3754</S> shall be wailing<S>4553</S>: for I will pass<S>5674</S> through<S>7130</S> thee, saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.

Woe<S>1945</S> unto you that desire<S>183</S> the day<S>3117</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>! to what end<S>4100</S> is it for you? the day<S>3117</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> is darkness<S>2822</S>, and not light<S>216</S>.

As if a man<S>376</S> did flee<S>5127</S> from<S>6440</S> a lion<S>738</S>, and a bear<S>1677</S> met<S>6293</S> him; or went<S>935</S> into the house<S>1004</S>, and leaned<S>5564</S> his hand<S>3027</S> on the wall<S>7023</S>, and a serpent<S>5175</S> bit<S>5391</S> him.
Shall not the day<S>3117</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> be darkness<S>2822</S>, and not light<S>216</S>? even very dark<S>651</S>, and no brightness<S>5051</S> in it?
I hate<S>8130</S>, I despise<S>3988</S> your feast days<S>2282</S>, and I will not smell<S>7306</S> in your solemn assemblies<S>6116</S>. <sup>in: or, your holy days</sup>

Though ye offer<S>5927</S> me burnt offerings<S>5930</S> and your meat offerings<S>4503</S>, I will not accept<S>7521</S> them: neither will I regard<S>5027</S> the peace offerings<S>8002</S> of your fat beasts<S>4806</S>. <sup>peace: or, thank offerings</sup>
Take thou away<S>5493</S> from me the noise<S>1995</S> of thy songs<S>7892</S>; for I will not hear<S>8085</S> the melody<S>2172</S> of thy viols<S>5035</S>.
But let judgment<S>4941</S> run down<S>1556</S> as waters<S>4325</S>, and righteousness<S>6666</S> as a mighty<S>386</S> stream<S>5158</S>. <sup>run: Heb. roll</sup>
Have ye offered<S>5066</S> unto me sacrifices<S>2077</S> and offerings<S>4503</S> in the wilderness<S>4057</S> forty<S>705</S> years<S>8141</S>, O house<S>1004</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>?

But ye have borne<S>5375</S> the tabernacle<S>5522</S> of your Moloch<S>4428</S> and Chiun<S>3594</S> your images<S>6754</S>, the star<S>3556</S> of your god<S>430</S>, which ye made<S>6213</S> to yourselves. <sup>the tabernacle: or, Siccuth your king</sup>
Therefore will I cause you to go into captivity<S>1540</S> beyond<S>1973</S> Damascus<S>1834</S>, saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, whose name<S>8034</S> is The God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>.

6.text: Woe<S>1945</S> to them that are at ease<S>7600</S> in Zion<S>6726</S>, and trust<S>982</S> in the mountain<S>2022</S> of Samaria<S>8111</S>, which are named<S>5344</S> chief<S>7225</S> of the nations<S>1471</S>, to whom the house<S>1004</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> came<S>935</S>! <sup>are at: or, are secure</sup> <sup>chief: or, firstfruits</sup>

Pass<S>5674</S> ye unto Calneh<S>3641</S>, and see<S>7200</S>; and from thence go<S>3212</S> ye to Hamath<S>2574</S> the great<S>7227</S> <S>2579</S>: then go down<S>3381</S> to Gath<S>1661</S> of the Philistines<S>6430</S>: be they better<S>2896</S> than these kingdoms<S>4467</S>? or their border<S>1366</S> greater<S>7227</S> than your border<S>1366</S>?
Ye that put far away<S>5077</S> the evil<S>7451</S> day<S>3117</S>, and cause the seat<S>7675</S> of violence<S>2555</S> to come near<S>5066</S>; <sup>seat: or, habitation</sup>

That lie<S>7901</S> upon beds<S>4296</S> of ivory<S>8127</S>, and stretch<S>5628</S> themselves upon their couches<S>6210</S>, and eat<S>398</S> the lambs<S>3733</S> out of the flock<S>6629</S>, and the calves<S>5695</S> out of the midst<S>8432</S> of the stall<S>4770</S>; <sup>stretch: or, abound with superfluities</sup>

That chant<S>6527</S> to the sound<S>6310</S> of the viol<S>5035</S>, and invent<S>2803</S> to themselves instruments<S>3627</S> of musick<S>7892</S>, like David<S>1732</S>; <sup>chant: or, quaver</sup>

That drink<S>8354</S> wine<S>3196</S> in bowls<S>4219</S>, and anoint<S>4886</S> themselves with the chief<S>7225</S> ointments<S>8081</S>: but they are not grieved<S>2470</S> for the affliction<S>7667</S> of Joseph<S>3130</S>. <sup>wine: or, in bowls of wine</sup> <sup>affliction: Heb. breach</sup>

Therefore now shall they go captive<S>1540</S> with the first<S>7218</S> that go captive<S>1540</S>, and the banquet<S>4797</S> of them that stretched<S>5628</S> themselves shall be removed<S>5493</S>.

The Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> hath sworn<S>7650</S> by himself<S>5315</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> the God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, I abhor<S>8374</S> the excellency<S>1347</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>, and hate<S>8130</S> his palaces<S>759</S>: therefore will I deliver up<S>5462</S> the city<S>5892</S> with all that is therein<S>4393</S>. <sup>that: Heb. the fulness thereof</sup>
And it shall come to pass, if there remain<S>3498</S> ten<S>6235</S> men<S>582</S> in one<S>259</S> house<S>1004</S>, that they shall die<S>4191</S>.

And a man's uncle<S>1730</S> shall take him up<S>5375</S>, and he that burneth<S>5635</S> him, to bring out<S>3318</S> the bones<S>6106</S> out of the house<S>1004</S>, and shall say<S>559</S> unto him that is by the sides<S>3411</S> of the house<S>1004</S>, Is there yet any with thee? and he shall say<S>559</S>, No<S>657</S>. Then shall he say<S>559</S>, Hold thy tongue<S>2013</S>: for we may not make mention<S>2142</S> of the name<S>8034</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>. <sup>we: or, they will not, or, have not</sup>

For, behold, the LORD<S>3068</S> commandeth<S>6680</S>, and he will smite<S>5221</S> the great<S>1419</S> house<S>1004</S> with breaches<S>7447</S>, and the little<S>6996</S> house<S>1004</S> with clefts<S>1233</S>. <sup>breaches: or, droppings</sup>

Shall horses<S>5483</S> run<S>7323</S> upon the rock<S>5553</S>? will one plow<S>2790</S> there with oxen<S>1241</S>? for ye have turned<S>2015</S> judgment<S>4941</S> into gall<S>7219</S>, and the fruit<S>6529</S> of righteousness<S>6666</S> into hemlock<S>3939</S>:
Ye which rejoice<S>8056</S> in a thing<S>1697</S> of nought<S>3808</S>, which say<S>559</S>, Have we not taken<S>3947</S> to us horns<S>7161</S> by our own strength<S>2392</S>?

But, behold, I will raise up<S>6965</S> against you a nation<S>1471</S>, O house<S>1004</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> the God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>; and they shall afflict<S>3905</S> you from the entering in<S>935</S> of Hemath<S>2574</S> unto the river<S>5158</S> of the wilderness<S>6160</S>. <sup>river: or, valley</sup>

7.text: Thus hath the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> shewed<S>7200</S> unto me; and, behold, he formed<S>3335</S> grasshoppers<S>1462</S> in the beginning<S>8462</S> of the shooting up<S>5927</S> of the latter growth<S>3954</S>; and, lo, it was the latter growth<S>3954</S> after<S>310</S> the king's<S>4428</S> mowings<S>1488</S>. <sup>grasshoppers: or, green worms</sup>

And it came to pass, that when they had made an end<S>3615</S> of eating<S>398</S> the grass<S>6212</S> of the land<S>776</S>, then I said<S>559</S>, O Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>, forgive<S>5545</S>, I beseech thee: by whom shall Jacob<S>3290</S> arise<S>6965</S>? for he is small<S>6996</S>. <sup>by: or, who of (or, for,) Jacob shall stand?</sup>

Thus hath the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> shewed<S>7200</S> unto me: and, behold, the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> called<S>7121</S> to contend<S>7378</S> by fire<S>784</S>, and it devoured<S>398</S> the great<S>7227</S> deep<S>8415</S>, and did eat up<S>398</S> a part<S>2506</S>.

Then said<S>559</S> I, O Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>, cease<S>2308</S>, I beseech thee: by whom shall Jacob<S>3290</S> arise<S>6965</S>? for he is small<S>6996</S>. <sup>by: or, who of (or, for,) Jacob shall stand?</sup>
The LORD<S>3068</S> repented<S>5162</S> for this: This also shall not be, saith<S>559</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>.
Thus he shewed<S>7200</S> me: and, behold, the Lord<S>136</S> stood<S>5324</S> upon a wall<S>2346</S> made by a plumbline<S>594</S>, with a plumbline<S>594</S> in his hand<S>3027</S>.

And the LORD<S>3068</S> said<S>559</S> unto me, Amos<S>5986</S>, what seest<S>7200</S> thou? And I said<S>559</S>, A plumbline<S>594</S>. Then said<S>559</S> the Lord<S>136</S>, Behold, I will set<S>7760</S> a plumbline<S>594</S> in the midst<S>7130</S> of my people<S>5971</S> Israel<S>3478</S>: I will not again<S>3254</S> pass by<S>5674</S> them any more:

And the high places<S>1116</S> of Isaac<S>3446</S> shall be desolate<S>8074</S>, and the sanctuaries<S>4720</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> shall be laid waste<S>2717</S>; and I will rise<S>6965</S> against the house<S>1004</S> of Jeroboam<S>3379</S> with the sword<S>2719</S>.

For thus Amos<S>5986</S> saith<S>559</S>, Jeroboam<S>3379</S> shall die<S>4191</S> by the sword<S>2719</S>, and Israel<S>3478</S> shall surely<S>1540</S> be led away captive<S>1540</S> out of their own land<S>127</S>.

Also Amaziah<S>558</S> said<S>559</S> unto Amos<S>5986</S>, O thou seer<S>2374</S>, go<S>3212</S>, flee thee away<S>1272</S> into the land<S>776</S> of Judah<S>3063</S>, and there eat<S>398</S> bread<S>3899</S>, and prophesy<S>5012</S> there:

Then answered<S>6030</S> Amos<S>5986</S>, and said<S>559</S> to Amaziah<S>558</S>, I was no prophet<S>5030</S>, neither was I a prophet's<S>5030</S> son<S>1121</S>; but I was an herdman<S>951</S>, and a gatherer<S>1103</S> of sycomore fruit<S>8256</S>: <sup>sycomore: or, wild figs</sup>

8.text: Thus hath the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> shewed<S>7200</S> unto me: and behold a basket<S>3619</S> of summer fruit<S>7019</S>.

And he said<S>559</S>, Amos<S>5986</S>, what seest<S>7200</S> thou? And I said<S>559</S>, A basket<S>3619</S> of summer fruit<S>7019</S>. And the songs<S>7892</S> of the temple<S>1964</S> shall be howlings<S>3213</S> in that day<S>3117</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>: there shall be many<S>7227</S> dead bodies<S>6297</S> in every place<S>4725</S>; they shall cast them forth<S>7993</S> with silence<S>2013</S>. <sup>shall be howlings: Heb. shall howl</sup> <sup>with: Heb. be silent</sup>
Hear<S>8085</S> this, O ye that swallow up<S>7602</S> the needy<S>34</S>, even to make<S>7673</S> the poor<S>6041</S> <S>6035</S> of the land<S>776</S> to fail<S>7673</S>,

Saying<S>559</S>, When will the new moon<S>2320</S> be gone<S>5674</S>, that we may sell<S>7666</S> corn<S>7668</S>? and the sabbath<S>7676</S>, that we may set forth<S>6605</S> wheat<S>1250</S>, making the ephah<S>374</S> small<S>6994</S>, and the shekel<S>8255</S> great<S>1431</S>, and falsifying<S>5791</S> the balances<S>3976</S> by deceit<S>4820</S>? <sup>new: or, month</sup> <sup>set: Heb. open</sup> <sup>falsifying: Heb. perverting the balances of deceit</sup>
That we may buy<S>7069</S> the poor<S>1800</S> for silver<S>3701</S>, and the needy<S>34</S> for a pair of shoes<S>5275</S>; yea, and sell<S>7666</S> the refuse<S>4651</S> of the wheat<S>1250</S>?

Shall not the land<S>776</S> tremble<S>7264</S> for this, and every one mourn<S>56</S> that dwelleth<S>3427</S> therein? and it shall rise up<S>5927</S> wholly as a flood<S>2975</S>; and it shall be cast out<S>1644</S> and drowned<S>8257</S> <S>8248</S>, as by the flood<S>216</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>.

And it shall come to pass in that day<S>3117</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>, that I will cause the sun<S>8121</S> to go down<S>935</S> at noon<S>6672</S>, and I will darken<S>2821</S> the earth<S>776</S> in the clear<S>216</S> day<S>3117</S>:

And I will turn<S>2015</S> your feasts<S>2282</S> into mourning<S>60</S>, and all your songs<S>7892</S> into lamentation<S>7015</S>; and I will bring up<S>5927</S> sackcloth<S>8242</S> upon all loins<S>4975</S>, and baldness<S>7144</S> upon every head<S>7218</S>; and I will make<S>7760</S> it as the mourning<S>60</S> of an only<S>3173</S> son, and the end<S>319</S> thereof as a bitter<S>4751</S> day<S>3117</S>.

Behold, the days<S>3117</S> come<S>935</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S>, that I will send<S>7971</S> a famine<S>7458</S> in the land<S>776</S>, not a famine<S>7458</S> of bread<S>3899</S>, nor a thirst<S>6772</S> for water<S>4325</S>, but of hearing<S>8085</S> the words<S>1697</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>:

And they shall wander<S>5128</S> from sea<S>3220</S> to sea<S>3220</S>, and from the north<S>6828</S> even to the east<S>4217</S>, they shall run to and fro<S>7751</S> to seek<S>1245</S> the word<S>1697</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>, and shall not find<S>4672</S> it.
In that day<S>3117</S> shall the fair<S>3303</S> virgins<S>1330</S> and young men<S>970</S> faint<S>5968</S> for thirst<S>6772</S>.

They that swear<S>7650</S> by the sin<S>819</S> of Samaria<S>8111</S>, and say<S>559</S>, Thy god<S>430</S>, O Dan<S>1835</S>, liveth<S>2416</S>; and, The manner<S>1870</S> of Beersheba<S>884</S> liveth<S>2416</S>; even they shall fall<S>5307</S>, and never rise up<S>6965</S> again. <sup>manner: Heb. way</sup>

9.text: I saw<S>7200</S> the Lord<S>136</S> standing<S>5324</S> upon the altar<S>4196</S>: and he said<S>559</S>, Smite<S>5221</S> the lintel of the door<S>3730</S>, that the posts<S>5592</S> may shake<S>7493</S>: and cut<S>1214</S> them in the head<S>7218</S>, all of them; and I will slay<S>2026</S> the last<S>319</S> of them with the sword<S>2719</S>: he that fleeth<S>5127</S> of them shall not flee away<S>5127</S>, and he that escapeth<S>6412</S> of them shall not be delivered<S>4422</S>. <sup>lintel: or, chapiter, or, knop</sup> <sup>cut: or, wound them</sup>

Though they dig<S>2864</S> into hell<S>7585</S>, thence shall mine hand<S>3027</S> take<S>3947</S> them; though they climb up<S>5927</S> to heaven<S>8064</S>, thence will I bring them down<S>3381</S>:

And though they hide<S>2244</S> themselves in the top<S>7218</S> of Carmel<S>3760</S>, I will search<S>2664</S> and take them out<S>3947</S> thence; and though they be hid<S>5641</S> from my sight<S>5869</S> in the bottom<S>7172</S> of the sea<S>3220</S>, thence will I command<S>6680</S> the serpent<S>5175</S>, and he shall bite<S>5391</S> them:

And though they go<S>3212</S> into captivity<S>7628</S> before<S>6440</S> their enemies<S>341</S>, thence will I command<S>6680</S> the sword<S>2719</S>, and it shall slay<S>2026</S> them: and I will set<S>7760</S> mine eyes<S>5869</S> upon them for evil<S>7451</S>, and not for good<S>2896</S>.

And the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> of hosts<S>6635</S> is he that toucheth<S>5060</S> the land<S>776</S>, and it shall melt<S>4127</S>, and all that dwell<S>3427</S> therein shall mourn<S>56</S>: and it shall rise up<S>5927</S> wholly like a flood<S>2975</S>; and shall be drowned<S>8257</S>, as by the flood<S>2975</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>.

It is he that buildeth<S>1129</S> his stories<S>4609</S> in the heaven<S>8064</S>, and hath founded<S>3245</S> his troop<S>92</S> in the earth<S>776</S>; he that calleth<S>7121</S> for the waters<S>4325</S> of the sea<S>3220</S>, and poureth them out<S>8210</S> upon the face<S>6440</S> of the earth<S>776</S>: The LORD<S>3068</S> is his name<S>8034</S>. <sup>stories: or, spheres: Heb. ascensions</sup> <sup>troop: or, bundle</sup>

Are ye not as children<S>1121</S> of the Ethiopians<S>3569</S> unto me, O children<S>1121</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>? saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>. Have not I brought up<S>5927</S> Israel<S>3478</S> out of the land<S>776</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>? and the Philistines<S>6430</S> from Caphtor<S>3731</S>, and the Syrians<S>758</S> from Kir<S>7024</S>?

Behold, the eyes<S>5869</S> of the Lord<S>136</S> GOD<S>3069</S> are upon the sinful<S>2403</S> kingdom<S>4467</S>, and I will destroy<S>8045</S> it from off the face<S>6440</S> of the earth<S>127</S>; saving<S>657</S> that I will not utterly<S>8045</S> destroy<S>8045</S> the house<S>1004</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>.

For, lo, I will command<S>6680</S>, and I will sift<S>5128</S> the house<S>1004</S> of Israel<S>3478</S> among all nations<S>1471</S>, like as corn is sifted<S>5128</S> in a sieve<S>3531</S>, yet shall not the least grain<S>6872</S> fall<S>5307</S> upon the earth<S>776</S>. <sup>sift: Heb. cause to move</sup> <sup>grain: Heb. stone</sup>
All the sinners<S>2400</S> of my people<S>5971</S> shall die<S>4191</S> by the sword<S>2719</S>, which say<S>559</S>, The evil<S>7451</S> shall not overtake<S>5066</S> nor prevent<S>6923</S> us.

In that day<S>3117</S> will I raise up<S>6965</S> the tabernacle<S>5521</S> of David<S>1732</S> that is fallen<S>5307</S>, and close up<S>1443</S> the breaches<S>6556</S> thereof; and I will raise up<S>6965</S> his ruins<S>2034</S>, and I will build<S>1129</S> it as in the days<S>3117</S> of old<S>5769</S>: <sup>close: Heb. hedge, or, wall</sup>

That they may possess<S>3423</S> the remnant<S>7611</S> of Edom<S>123</S>, and of all the heathen<S>1471</S>, which are called<S>7121</S> by my name<S>8034</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> that doeth<S>6213</S> this. <sup>which: Heb. upon whom my name is called</sup>

Behold, the days<S>3117</S> come<S>935</S>, saith<S>5002</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, that the plowman<S>2790</S> shall overtake<S>5066</S> the reaper<S>7114</S>, and the treader<S>1869</S> of grapes<S>6025</S> him that soweth<S>4900</S> seed<S>2233</S>; and the mountains<S>2022</S> shall drop<S>5197</S> sweet wine<S>6071</S>, and all the hills<S>1389</S> shall melt<S>4127</S>. <sup>soweth: Heb. draweth forth</sup> <sup>sweet: or, new</sup>

And I will bring again<S>7725</S> the captivity<S>7622</S> of my people<S>5971</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, and they shall build<S>1129</S> the waste<S>8074</S> cities<S>5892</S>, and inhabit<S>3427</S> them; and they shall plant<S>5193</S> vineyards<S>3754</S>, and drink<S>8354</S> the wine<S>3196</S> thereof; they shall also make<S>6213</S> gardens<S>1593</S>, and eat<S>398</S> the fruit<S>6529</S> of them.

And I will plant<S>5193</S> them upon their land<S>127</S>, and they shall no more be pulled up<S>5428</S> out of their land<S>127</S> which I have given<S>5414</S> them, saith<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> thy God<S>430</S>.
